Prince George County VA

Asphalt Paving
Prince George County VA

Serving Fort Gregg-Adams, South Prince George & surrounding areas — ~40 miles from Chester

Get a Free Estimate 📞 (804) 446-1296
🏆 40+ Years Experience ✅ Licensed & Insured ⭐ 4.8-Star Rated 📋 Free Estimates

Prince George County VA's Professional Paving Contractor

Prince George County occupies a unique position in the greater Richmond-Hampton Roads corridor — adjacent to Hopewell and Petersburg, home to Fort Gregg-Adams (formerly Fort Lee) and its significant military and civilian community, and increasingly suburban along the Route 460 corridor. The county's diverse mix of residential neighborhoods, agricultural land, and commercial development creates an equally diverse range of paving needs. J. Worden & Sons has been serving property owners in Prince George County from our Chester VA base for decades, bringing the expertise of a 4th-generation family business to every driveway, parking lot, and commercial paving project we take on.

Prince George County's soils present an interesting transition. Western areas of the county tend toward Piedmont clay, requiring careful drainage engineering to prevent sub-base instability. Eastern areas shift toward the coastal plain with sandier, better-draining soils — but these require adequate aggregate base depth to remain stable under traffic loads. Agricultural areas throughout the county may have compacted or disturbed subsoil from farming that requires additional excavation before paving. Our process addresses each site individually: we evaluate the soil profile, design the appropriate base, and engineer drainage to protect your investment for decades. The military families near Fort Gregg-Adams know us for our punctuality and precision — we treat your project with the same respect you'd give any high-standards endeavor.

Services in Prince George County VA

  • Asphalt driveway paving & full replacement
  • Commercial parking lot paving
  • Sealcoating — driveways & parking lots
  • Pothole patching & crack sealing
  • Grading, excavation & base preparation
  • Parking lot line striping & ADA compliance
  • Chip & tar surface treatment
  • Concrete, cobblestone & stone masonry

Asphalt Paving Prince George County VA — FAQs

How much does asphalt paving cost in Prince George County VA?

Asphalt paving in Prince George County VA runs $3–$7 per square foot for residential driveways. A standard two-car driveway (600–800 sq ft) typically costs $2,500–$6,000. Commercial parking lots are quoted individually by scope and site conditions. We travel from Chester VA — approximately 40 miles — with no travel surcharge. All estimates are free, written, and in-person. Call (804) 446-1296 to schedule.

Do you serve the Fort Gregg-Adams community and surrounding neighborhoods?

Yes — we regularly serve residents and property owners near Fort Gregg-Adams, South Prince George, Burrowsville, Disputanta, and the Prince George Courthouse area. Military families and veterans are valued customers we take pride in serving. We understand the high standards and scheduling expectations of military households. Whether it's a residential driveway or a commercial property on Route 460, we're ready to help.

What are the paving considerations near Prince George County's clay soils?

Prince George County has Piedmont clay transitioning to coastal plain sandy soils moving east. Clay-dominant areas require deeper excavation and careful drainage to prevent sub-base instability from seasonal moisture cycles. Eastern portions with sandier soils drain better but may be less stable as a base without adequate aggregate depth. We assess each site's soil profile individually and design base preparation accordingly — this site-specific approach is the foundation of durable results.

How long does the paving process take for a typical driveway in Prince George County?

A standard residential driveway replacement in Prince George County takes 1–2 days from start to finish. Day one typically covers demolition and base preparation; day two covers paving. The asphalt needs 24–48 hours to cure before driving on it and 3–5 days to reach full hardness. Larger driveways or projects requiring significant grading take longer. We provide a project timeline with your estimate so you can plan accordingly.

Can you handle commercial parking lot paving along the Route 460 corridor?

Absolutely. The Route 460 corridor in Prince George County is an active commercial zone and we're equipped to handle parking lot paving, resurfacing, and striping for businesses throughout this area. Commercial projects include full site evaluation, drainage engineering, ADA-compliant design, line striping, and phased construction to minimize business disruption. Contact us for a commercial site visit and free estimate.
